6	
7	### Please re-state the problem that we are trying to solve in this issue.
8	- There is no way to remove a unwanted stop from waypoints.
9	
10	### What is the root cause of that problem?
11	- We don't have any UI element to remove a stop from waypoints.
12	
13	### What changes do you think we should make in order to solve the problem?
14	- To allow stop deletion from three dot menu from inside waypoint address enter screen:
15	    - On three-dot-menu press we will open a modal and show single option "Remove Stop".
16	    - On press of "Remove Stop" we will open a delete confirmation modal to confirm the deletion of the stop and we will call `removeWaypoint` function on confirmation.
17	
18	(Not posting much details on code as its already gonna be the same.)
19	
20	- To allow stop deletion from list of stops:
21	    - We can add a `(-)` minus circle icon instead of showing the `Expensicons.DotIndicator` icon.
22	    - We will also apply icon theme as `theme.danger`. To notify user that this is a remove icon.
23	    - Then we can make that icon pressable in `MenuItem.js` so that we can open a modal to confirm the deletion of the stop and we can call `removeWaypoint` function on confirmation.
24	
25	- Why two different approaches?
26	    - As user might have added a stop by mistake and he wants to remove it and we don't want to make user go inside the address enter screen to remove the stop. So we are providing a way to remove stop from list of stops.
27	
28	- Further Enhancements:
29	    - Also if we are calling same modal from two screens we can create a common delete stop modal file and use it in both the screens. To eliminate code duplication.
30	
31	    - Further I was thinking of making a common modal where we can pass title, description and button names so that we can create functions for modal instead of writing code for `ConfirmModal` everywhere, we are doing a similar thing using `ReportActionContextMenu.showDeleteModal` which eliminates code duplication.

178	## Proposal 4

180	## Proposal
181	
182	### Please re-state the problem that we are trying to solve in this issue.
183	Make it Easier to delete waypoints.
184	
185	### What is the root cause of that problem?
186	This is a new feature
187	
188	### What changes do you think we should make in order to solve the problem?
189	Here are the steps we should take to implement this feature:
190	
191	- Add the `shouldShowThreeDotsButton` button to `Header with back button` component [here](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/f48a2085d2e22bc011c4028fce56413d088b7896/src/pages/iou/WaypointEditor.js#L116), this will ensure we have the three dots menu.
192	- Add a state variable on the page [here](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/f48a2085d2e22bc011c4028fce56413d088b7896/src/pages/iou/WaypointEditor.js#L60) to track if the modal is open. we will name it `isModalVisible` and set the default value false.
193	  ```js
194	  const [isDeleteModalVisible, setIsDeleteModalVisible] = useState(false);
195	  ```
196	- Add the custom menu item to a const variable:
197		```js
198		const threeDotsMenuItems = {
199	        icon: Expensicons.Trashcan,
200	        translationKey: 'common.delete',
201	        action: () => setIsDeleteModalVisible(true)
202		}
203		```
204		This has the common delete text and pressing it sets the Delete Modal Visibility to `true`. We will now pass this as a prop to HeaderWithBackButton:
205		```js
206		<HeaderWithBackButton
207		      threeDotsMenuItems={threeDotsMenuItems}
208		      ...
209		/>
210		```
211	- Add the `ConfirmModal` component to the code [here](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/f48a2085d2e22bc011c4028fce56413d088b7896/src/pages/iou/WaypointEditor.js#L155) and make sure it is visible only while the variable we defined has the `true` value.
212		```js
213		{isDeleteModalVisible &&
214			<ConfirmModal
215	    		title={props.translate('waypoint.common.delete')}
216	    		isVisible={isDeleteModalOpen}
217	    		onConfirm={deleteWaypointAndHideModal}
218	    		onCancel={() => setIsDeleteModalVisible(false)}
219	    		prompt={props.translate('waypoint.common.deleteConfirmation')}
220	    		confirmText={props.translate('common.delete')}
221	    		cancelText={props.translate('common.cancel')}
222	    		danger
223			/>
224		}
225		```
226	
227	- Now we will be defining the `deleteWaypointAndHideModal` function which will be using the `removeWaypoint` function from [here](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/252cc247dbeed5b7e4b6cbaa15f91a2bdb20b520/src/libs/actions/Transaction.js#L92) (passing it the `transactionID from props` and the `waypointIndex` from the route params) and will hide the modal using `setIsDeleteModalVisible(false)`
228	
229	-  We will be adding the translations [here](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/0b7ae7f58e3a98ba49cf9fc2812921964d9d8895/src/languages/en.js#L6) and [here](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/0b7ae7f58e3a98ba49cf9fc2812921964d9d8895/src/languages/es.js#L5) under a `waypoint.common:` key
230	
231	### What alternative solutions did you explore? (Optional)

text:
Let me analyze all five proposals for issue 25901 and decide which one should be accepted as a maintainer would.

**The problem:** Make it easier to delete waypoints. Currently, users can only delete a waypoint by saving it with an empty text input â€” not discoverable. The desired flow is: dot menu icon â†’ menu popup â†’ delete confirmation.

**Proposal analysis:**

### Proposal 0
- Suggests **two approaches**: (1) Three-dot menu inside address editor with "Remove Stop" â†’ confirmation modal â†’ `removeWaypoint`, and (2) A `(-)` minus icon in the list of stops view.
- Also suggests making a common delete stop modal component.
- The two-approach idea goes beyond the scope. The issue specifically asks for "Dot menu icon â†’ menu pop up â†’ delete confirmation." The second approach (minus icon in the list) is not what was requested and adds unnecessary UI surface.
- The proposal also doesn't mention using `threeDotsMenuItems` or `shouldShowThreeDotsButton` on `HeaderWithBackButton` specifically, which is the existing pattern in the codebase.

### Proposal 1
- Very brief. Mentions adding `shouldShowThreeDotsButton` to `HeaderWithBackButton`.
- Uses `Trashcan` icon and `delete` text.
- Uses `ConfirmModal`.
- Very sparse on details â€” doesn't explain translations, navigation after deletion, or the `removeWaypoint` call specifics.

### Proposal 2
- Mentions tracking whether a waypoint exists to decide whether to show the three-dot menu.
- Suggests using `threeDotMenuItems` on `HeaderWithBackButton`.
- Uses `ConfirmModal` in `WaypointEditor`.
- Mentions navigation after deletion (go back).
- More detailed than 1 but still somewhat brief. Notes need for `threeDotsAnchorPosition` and `withWindowDimensions` HOC for desktop support â€” good attention to detail.

### Proposal 3
- Most detailed and well-structured:
  1. Props `shouldShowThreeDotsButton` and `threeDotsMenuItems` to `HeaderWithBackButton`
  2. Menu item with `Trashcan` icon, `common.delete` text, opens confirmation
  3. `ConfirmModal` with specific props including `danger` prop
  4. `confirmDeleteAndHideModal` function that calls `Transaction.removeWaypoint`
  5. Mentions new translations for waypoint delete
  6. Provides a complete working video
- References actual code locations precisely.
- Covers translations, confirm modal props, and the actual delete action.

### Proposal 4
- Similar to 3 but slightly different implementation details.
- Uses `translationKey` instead of `text` for the menu item.
- Suggests translations under `waypoint.common:` key.
- Also a solid proposal but less precise about the actual HeaderWithBackButton API.

**Decision:**

Proposal 3 is the strongest. It:
1. Identifies the correct existing patterns (`shouldShowThreeDotsButton`, `threeDotsMenuItems`, `ConfirmModal` with `danger` prop)
2. References the exact code locations
3. Shows a complete working solution (video evidence)
4. Covers translations
5. Handles the complete flow: three-dot â†’ menu â†’ confirm â†’ delete â†’ navigate back
6. Is the most thorough and precise in its explanation of what code changes are needed

Proposal 0 goes beyond scope (two approaches, minus icon in list). Proposal 1 is too sparse. Proposal 2 is decent but less comprehensive. Proposal 4 is solid but gets the `translationKey` vs `text` distinction wrong (the `HeaderWithBackButton` uses `text` not `translationKey` for three-dot menu items, and even if it uses i18n internally, the other proposals' use of `translate('common.delete')` is more aligned with how the codebase works).

Let me select Proposal 3 (ID 3).

Now let me examine the proposals against the issue requirements:

**Issue (#25901):** Add a "dot menu icon â†’ menu popup â†’ delete confirmation" flow for deleting waypoints in the waypoint editor.

Let me evaluate each proposal:

**Proposal 0** â€” Suggests **two** approaches: (1) three-dot menu in the address editor, and (2) a `(-)` minus-circle icon on the list view. The second approach goes beyond the requested scope. Also suggests building a generic reusable modal, which is scope creep. The issue asks for a specific flow, not a platform refactor.

**Proposal 1** â€” Too sparse on implementation detail. Mentions `shouldShowThreeDotsButton` and `ConfirmModal` but gives no specifics on navigation after deletion, translations, or the `removeWaypoint` call.

**Proposal 2** â€” Better. Notes the need for `threeDotsAnchorPosition` and `withWindowDimensions` for desktop support â€” good attention to detail. But still relatively brief on the complete flow and doesn't detail translations.

**Proposal 3** â€” Most thorough. References exact code locations, specifies `ConfirmModal` props including `danger`, covers translations (`deleteStop`, `deleteStopConfirmation`), defines the `confirmDeleteAndHideModal` function calling `Transaction.removeWaypoint`, and includes a working video demonstration. Follows existing codebase patterns correctly.

**Proposal 4** â€” Similar to 3 but uses `translationKey` instead of `text` for the menu item, which doesn't match how `HeaderWithBackButton`'s three-dot menu API works in this codebase.

text: The file is written. Let me summarize my decision.

**Decision:** Selected **Proposal 3**.

**Rationale:**

Proposal 3 is the strongest on all dimensions a maintainer cares about:

1. **Scope discipline** â€” Stays exactly within the requested flow (dot menu â†’ popup â†’ confirmation â†’ delete). Doesn't tack on unrelated UI changes (like Proposal 0's minus-icon-on-list approach) or generic infrastructure work.

2. **Correct API usage** â€” Correctly identifies `shouldShowThreeDotsButton`, `threeDotsMenuItems`, and `ConfirmModal` with the `danger` prop â€” matching the existing codebase patterns.

3. **Completeness** â€” Covers the full end-to-end flow:
   - Adding the three-dot menu to `HeaderWithBackButton`
   - Wiring `onSelected` to open `ConfirmModal`
   - The `confirmDeleteAndHideModal` handler calling `Transaction.removeWaypoint(transactionID, waypointIndex)`
   - Proper translations for the modal title and prompt
   - Working video demonstration

4. **Precision** â€” References exact code locations (`WaypointEditor.js` line ~116, `Transaction.js` line ~92) rather than vague "we should add X somewhere."

5. **Production quality** â€” Handles edge states the others miss: the `danger` prop on the confirm button (signals destructive action correctly), the modal title/body as separate translations, and navigation back after deletion.
